title: TIE Avenger prototype
content:
{{Top|legends=TIE/ad starfighter}} {{Otheruses|Avenger}} {{Split}} {{Update|{{ILM|url=andor-star-wars-tie-avenger-ilm-vfx|text=Assembling a Starfighter: Exploring ILM's Role in Creating the TIE Avenger from 'Andor'}}}} {{StarshipClass |image=[[File:Tie_avenger_one_year_later.png]] |name=TIE Avenger prototype |designer= |manufacturer=[[Sienar Fleet Systems]]{{Andor|One Year Later}} |line=[[TIE series]]{{EncyclopediaCite|TIE Fighters}} |model=TIE avenger |type=[[Starfighter]] |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length=13.41 [[meter]]s{{Databank|tie-avenger-prototype|TIE Avenger prototype}} |width=7.49 meters |height=5.13 meters |mass= |max accel= |mglt=140 [[Megalight per hour|MGLT]][[Star Wars Encyclopedia of Starfighters and Other Vehicles (second edition)|''Star Wars Encyclopedia of Starfighters and Other Vehicles'' second edition]] |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine=Rear-mounted [[engine]]s (2) |hyperdrive=Class 1 |hdrange= |hdsystem=[[Hyperdrive|Equipped]] |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen=Equipped |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=*Double [[heavy laser cannon]] [[turret]] (1) *Rotary [[laser cannon]]s (2) {{C|retractable}} *Emission-style [[proton torpedo launcher]] (1) {{C|retractable}} *Light laser cannons (16) {{C|wing-mounted}} *Medium laser cannons (2) {{C|chin-mounted}} *Light [[warhead]] launchers (12) *Medium warhead launchers (4) |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew=1 [[pilot]] |skeleton= |passengers=At least 2 |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport=[[Life support|Equipped]] |communications= |othersystems=*Armoured [[Solar collector|solar gather array]] *Headlights (4) |availability= |role= |firstuse= |retired= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire]] **[[Imperial Starfighter Corps]] *[[Axis network]] {{C|stolen}} }} The '''TIE Avenger prototype''' was a model of [[TIE series]] [[starfighter]] that was used by the [[Galactic Empire]]. The manufactured prototypes of the [[TIE Advanced x1]] inspired future development of starfighters, which resulted in the creation of the [[TIE/IN interceptor]] and the TIE Avenger. The wings of [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter]] appeared similar to those found on the TIE Avenger and TIE/IN interceptor, with the difference being that the Defender's wings were angled outwards rather than inwards. ==Description== {{Quote|What do you think? Can you fly it?''"
"''I don't know. It's all new.|Bardi and Rika|}} [[File:TIEAvengerInterior-Parallax.png|left|thumb|300px|The TIE Avenger's cockpit was more expansive than other TIE models.]] The TIE Avenger prototype was an experimental [[TIE series]] [[starfighter]] developed for the [[Galactic Empire]] and tested by [[Sienar Fleet Systems]]. It was heavily armed and [[armor|armored]], carrying an externally-mounted port side dual [[laser cannon]], two anti-personnel rotary [[blaster cannon]]s, and wing-mounted [[missile]]s and [[torpedo|torpedoes]]. It also carried a heavier retractable heavy laser cannon under the cockpit pod, which, despite requiring some time to fully power-up, could punch through [[blast door]] armor with a single shot and incinerate targets. The craft was also equipped with an advanced [[targeting computer]] which could track hostile [[starship]]s and life signs and assist the [[pilot]] in taking them down using a [[hologram|holographic]] [[heads-up display]] projected in front of the cockpit [[viewport]]. The targeting system could also guide the ship's missiles on a target without user input. Unlike most TIE series starfighters,''[[TIE Fighter Owners' Workshop Manual]]'' the Avenger was equipped with a [[hyperdrive]], which allowed it to travel from [[star system|system]] to system.{{Andor|Harvest}} It also boasted two powerful rear-mounted [[engine]]s, controlled by an unusual control system which could be confusing even to experienced pilots if not acquainted with it. The ship shared aspects its wing design with those of the [[TIE/IN interceptor]], as well as the [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter]]. Due to the exorbitant cost of manufacture, the TIE Avenger was never put into full mass production.{{BuildFalconCite|48|Starship Fact File}} ==History== ===Development process, enter Andor=== {{Quote|How's it look?''"
"''It's good. It's prepped for a test flight in the morning. The password tonight is Kafrene.''"
"''Kafrene. Got the key?''"
"''We have to wait for my supervisor to leave. He never stays late.|Cassian Andor and Niya|audio=Andor-Niya-Key-OneYearLater.flac|}} Imperial engineers were intent on improving the TIE fighter design, and conceptualized a variety of iterations for the craft. The manufactured prototypes of the [[TIE Advanced x1]] inspired further evolutions of TIE starfighters, leading to the creation of the [[TIE/IN interceptor]], which had an [[TIE/IN Interceptor Prototype|operational prototype model]]''[[Star Wars: Galaxy of Heroes]]'' by [[14 BBY]],''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' and the TIE Avenger. In [[4 BBY]], a prototype of the TIE Avenger was under development at [[Sienar Test Facility 73]] by Sienar Fleet Systems on the company-owned planet of [[73]].{{Databank|sienar-test-facility|Sienar Test Facility}}
[[File:Tie_avenger_rear.png|thumb|300px|Cassian Andor prepares to board the TIE Avenger prototype via its rear ramp]]
The rebel [[Spy|agent]] [[Cassian Jeron Andor|Cassian Andor]] [[Theft of the TIE Avenger prototype|infiltrated]] the facility with the help of a technician named [[Niya]] in order to steal the TIE Avenger prototype. On account of its unusual non-standard flight controls, Andor had some initial struggles with piloting it, which saw him flying in reverse, setting off the alarm. After finally being able to fly out of the facility, the TIE Avenger lost power as it slid to a stop at the top of a canyon. Andor narrowly managed to restore power and dove to avoid a pair of torpedoes fired by two [[TIE/ad v1]]s. After a brief chase through the canyon while accidentally colliding with the walls, he was able to shake his pursuers by collapsing an ice tunnel on them and fled 73. ===The Yavin 4 misadventure=== [[File:TIE-Avenger-Theft.png|left|thumb|300px|Andor fires the Avenger's rotary cannons at responding stormtroopers]] He escaped to his remote rendezvous point on [[Yavin 4]], where he intended to hand it off to the rebel agent [[Porko]]. However, when Andor arrived, he did not find his contact, as Porko had been [[Death|killed]] during a misunderstanding with the remnants of a rebel group known as the [[Maya Pei Brigade]] who had, by chance been using the same location as a rally point after taking heavy losses from an Imperial ambush. The surviving members of the brigade, numbering fifteen individuals, proceeded to take Andor prisoner and investigate the TIE Avenger. The group hoped to use the ship to get off-planet, although their only qualified pilot, [[Rika]], found herself unable to operate the unusual control systems, even misfiring its main weapon while testing it out. The TIE Avenger also did not have enough space inside to fit all fifteen individuals. As the Maya Pei Brigade, short on food, water, and other supplies devolved into arguments about how to proceed, the rebel group soon divided into two sub-factions and turned on each other, with [[Bardi]]'s faction holding the TIE Avenger while [[Gerdis]]' faction took shelter in the wrecked [[Breon HOY-39 Dayvan]] ship, with Andor as their hostage.{{Andor|Sagrona Teema}} [[File:TIEAvengerYavin4-Parallax.png|right|thumb|300px|Andor took the prototype to Yavin 4 to rendezvous with his contact.]] Bardi's group later attempted to physically pull the TIE Avenger, rotating it in place on the forest floor, in order to point its main weapons at the other side's hideout. As Andor predicted they would likely make 5 meters by midnight, Gerdis' people to open fire at the TIE Avenger, forcing Bardi's people to take cover. At nightfall, Gerdis argued the logic of his plan, saying it was unwise to kill Andor (the only person who could fly the starfighter) in the process of killing everyone else. Eventually, both sides agreed to meet unarmed in order to resolve the conflict. The two sides agreed to [[Five Hands]] under [[Roski rules]]. In the subsequent chaos from Bardi and Gerdis being attacked and killed by a sudden attack by large [[Yavinian doodar]] emerging from the treeline, Andor managed to arm himself, retrieving both a blaster and an encrypted hyperwave communicator from a secret compartment on Porko's ship, and freeing himself from his chains. Sneaking back to the TIE Avenger during the chaos of the predator attack, he kills a member of the Maya Pei Brigade who had remained in the cockpit, before flying the fighter off-planet as the stranded Brigade members futilely tried to shoot it down with small arms blaster fire. ===Journey to Mina-Rau=== {{Quote|What's your location?''"
"''I'm in transit.''"
"''And where's our freighter? We've had no word from the relay pilot.''"
"''You won't be hearing from him. It's just me. I'm calling you from the freighter.''"
"''Let's start over. What's your location?''"
"''This is not the ship I was trained to fly, did you know that? Did ''anyone'' know that? I can't fly the ship, the drop is compromised. I've been upside-down for two days!|Kleya Marki and Cassian Andor|}} [[File:TIEAvengerTargetPractice-Harvest.png|thumb|300px|left|Andor fires on Imperial troops and stormtroopers on Mina-Rau.]] Once safely in [[Realspace|deep space]], Andor got to work trying to rewire the TIE Avenger to power the encrypted hyperwave communicator he had retrieved from Porko's ship, only managing to accidentally ignite the starboard thruster, sending him spinning out of control. Two [[Standard day|days]] later, he managed to successfully establish contact with [[Kleya Marki]] on [[Coruscant]], reporting the botched rendezvous and requesting a new drop-off location. Although Andor wanted to call his friends on [[Mina-Rau]], Marki informed him that there was a signal blockade on that planet, prompting Andor to travel there in order to discover what was happening. Exiting hyperspace above Mina-Rau, Andor spotted an orbiting Imperial cruiser and immediately performed a nosedive through the [[atmosphere]]. Using the TIE Avenger, he killed several Imperials investigating the farm where his friends were staying, before landing to find his friend [[Brasso]] had already been killed by a [[stormtrooper]]. [[Bix Caleen]] and [[Wilmon Paak]], realizing that Andor was piloting the TIE Avenger, arrived at his location and soon boarded the craft as the trio departed Mina-Rau. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|I can't begin to explain just how big it was. The wings were enormous. We made the body sectionable so that we could unbolt and re-bolt it. It could sit on its wings. We could fly it with a 100-ton crane. We could move it around. It had fully practical steps on the back. We could take the pod off on its own and put it on a gimbal for our gimbal work. It really felt like everyone that was involved in that knew they were involved in making something quite special.|Luke Murphy, on the practical TIE Avenger set|{{BookCite|title=befores & afters|number=#33|date=2025-06-17|pages=p. 20|isbn=979-8287898458}}}} [[File:Tie_avenger_bts.png|right|thumb|250px|The physical TIE Avenger prop being assembled]] The TIE Avenger prototype Cassian Andor stole was a rare full interior and exterior TIE Fighter set build. It could support its own weight on four points of its lower wings. The prototype's design was, in part, initially inspired by Kylo Ren's [[Kylo Ren's TIE whisper|TIE Whisper]] from ''[[Star Wars: Episode IX The Rise of Skywalker]]''. The TIE Avenger was first featured in the [[1994]] [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|TIE Fighter computer game]] from [[LucasArts]].{{SW|url=news/andor-trivia-guide-week-1|text=Secrets from the Set of ''Andor'' Season 2: Week 1 Trivia}} ==Appearances== *{{Andor|One Year Later}} {{1st}} *{{Andor|Sagrona Teema}} *{{Andor|Harvest}} ==Sources== *{{BuildFalconCite|17|Starship Fact File}} {{1stm}} *{{BuildFalconCite|48|Starship Fact File}} *{{EncyclopediaCite|TIE Fighters}} *{{DisneyPlusYouTube|gnwpxgR3STE|Coming in 2025 | Disney+|nolive=1}} *{{SWYouTube|AE4wxt70aUM|Andor | Season 2 Trailer | Streaming April 22 on Disney+}} *{{SWYouTube|feJSTZRRaFQ|Andor Season 2 | Special Look | Streaming April 22 on Disney+|int=Andor Season 2: A Special Look}} *{{SWYouTube|1Cz6h4FeTsk|Andor | Stealing the TIE Fighter | Disney+}} *{{SW|url=news/andor-concept-to-screen|text=Taking ''Andor'' from Concept to Screen}} *{{SW|url=news/andor-trivia-guide-week-1|text=Secrets from the Set of ''Andor'' Season 2: Week 1 Trivia}} *{{ILM|url=andor-star-wars-tie-avenger-ilm-vfx|text=Assembling a Starfighter: Exploring ILM's Role in Creating the TIE Avenger from 'Andor'}} *{{Databank|tie-avenger-prototype|TIE Avenger prototype}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |fr=Prototype de TIE Avenger |ka=TIE შურისმაძიებლის პროტოტიპი }} [[Category:Starfighter classes]] [[Category:Starship classes of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:TIE prototypes]]
